# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Docker Compose configuration for a MongoDB development environment. Define MongoDB service with exposed ports for application connectivity. Set up volume mounts for data persistence. This is for our local development and testing setup.
# Category: mongodb_exposed_port

version: '3'  # Specify the version of docker compose file format to use (default value as specified in current Docker Compose specification)
services:   # Define services which will be included within this configuration block, each service is defined by a key-value pair. The keys are named and values can either specify an image or set up environment variables for the container using `environment` attribute – both of them must follow in order to create valid docker compose file
    mongo:   # Name given to this Docker Compose service (mongo) is defined here as a MongoDB database. The value can be any name you want, but it should match the one used when defining your application services below under `services` key in main configuration block – both of them must follow
      image: mongo   # Specify Docker Image to use (default is 'mongo' if not specified)
      ports:    # Define how exposed port(s), and which ones, should be accessible from outside the container. The value can either specify a single number or an array of numbers – both must follow in order for this configuration block/service pair to create valid docker compose file (e.g., `ports` : [ "3027:27017" ] )
        - '3027:27017'   # Expose port ‘3027’ inside the container and map it on host to a specific external/internal IP address (default is none, meaning any available network interface) or use an internal Docker bridge. The value can be either single number for one-way communication between containers within same docker stack ('localhost')
      volumes:    # Define how data should persist in the container and where it's stored on host machine – both must follow to create valid configuration block/service pair (e.g., `volumes` : [ "/data:/db" ] )   This will mount a directory from your local system into Docker image at /db location
        - '/mongo-data':    # Name given for this volume, can be any name you want – it must match the one used when defining services below under `services` key in main configuration block (both of them need to follow)   This will persist data from host machine into Docker image at /db location
